title: Verilator Unexpected Crash at traceAll
body:
Hello 

A project that uses an embedded FPGA with adjustable tile size has been under my verification. There were no issues when verifying low tile sizes. But when tile sizes grow, designs become more intricate, and Verilator crashes as shown in the part below: 

```
- V3GraphAlg.cpp:462: Order:
- V3OrderProcessDomains.cpp:82:  Domains...
- V3OrderSerial.cpp:37:  Constructing serial code for 'stl'- V3SchedPartition.cpp:334:partition: 
- V3GraphAlg.cpp:462: Order:
- V3OrderProcessDomains.cpp:82:  Domains...
- V3OrderSerial.cpp:37:  Constructing serial code for 'ico'- V3GraphAlg.cpp:462: Order:
- V3OrderProcessDomains.cpp:82:  Domains...
- V3OrderSerial.cpp:37:  Constructing serial code for 'act'- V3GraphAlg.cpp:462: Order:
- V3OrderProcessDomains.cpp:82:  Domains...
- V3OrderSerial.cpp:37:  Constructing serial code for 'nba'- V3Clock.cpp:229:    clockAll: 
- V3Const.cpp:3988:   constifyAll: 
- V3Life.cpp:497:     lifeAll: 
- V3LifePost.cpp:362: lifepostAll: 
- V3Const.cpp:3988:   constifyAll: 
- V3Dead.cpp:568:     deadifyAllScoped: 
- V3Trace.cpp:913:    traceAll: 
%Error: export VERILATOR_ROOT=/home/dguzel/verilator
%Error: ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec /home/dguzel/verilator/bin/verilator_bin_dbg -cc -CFLAGS -std=c++14 --exe --x-assign 0 -Wno-lint --trace --assert --build -j --debug --no-debug-leak -no-dump-tree --top-module fpga_top --autoflush -F ../Fabric/SRC/fabric_netlists.flist verilator-main.cpp
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec /home/dguzel/verilator/bin/verilator_bin_dbg -cc -CFLAGS -std=c++14 --exe --x-assign 0 -Wno-lint --trace --assert --build -j --debug --no-debug-leak -no-dump-tree --top-module fpga_top --autoflush -F ../Fabric/SRC/fabric_netlists.flist verilator-main.cpp
Makefile:63: recipe for target 'verilate' failed
```

I have pasted here last part of the log file which is generated debug flag on.

During the Verilation process, Verilator consumes approximately 200GB of system memory—about 90% of the total available resources—and utilizes 100% of the CPU. This intensive resource consumption continues for nearly 90 minutes before the process ultimately crashes.

I have enabled the debug flag, and while the log file was generated, it does not provide any specific error messages other than three unspecified ones at the end of the process. Additionally, I have already set the terminal ulimit to unlimited, so I believe that the crash might be due to the unsufficent source.

Could you please clarify whether this crash is indeed a result of the design's complexity? If not, could you help me understand the underlying cause and suggest ways to optimize the process to prevent Verilator from crashing?

Thank you for your assistance.
